expectations.text: Output contains a properly structured search matrix with Context, Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 sections
expectations.evidence: Output is a phased research plan, not a tiered search matrix. No Context/Tier structure present

expectations.text: Matrix uses at least 3 different engine categories including legal or policy databases
expectations.evidence: No engine categories specified. Search terms are listed as plain text without target engine designation

expectations.text: Search queries use engine-specific operators such as site:, filetype:pdf, or date range filters
expectations.evidence: No engine-specific operators used. All queries are natural language phrases

expectations.text: Tier 2 queries broaden scope compared to Tier 1, and Tier 3 explores alternative or lateral sources
expectations.evidence: Phases are organized by jurisdiction (EU, US, China), not by search scope escalation

expectations.text: Each row includes specific acceptance criteria for evaluating search results
expectations.evidence: No per-query acceptance criteria. Phases list key questions but not measurable acceptance thresholds

expectations.text: Output includes a Runtime Recovery section with fallback strategies
expectations.evidence: No Runtime Recovery section. A Risks section discusses general research risks but not search fallback strategies

expectations.text: Matrix covers regulatory frameworks from at least EU, US, and China
expectations.evidence: Phases 1-3 explicitly cover EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, and China Interim Measures

expectations.text: At least one query specifically targets enforcement actions or audit case studies
expectations.evidence: Phase 4 includes search terms for 'AI regulation enforcement action' and 'LLM regulatory audit'

expectations.text: Output includes a Grading Summary section with per-tier pass/fail thresholds
expectations.evidence: No formal Grading Summary, but the timeline section provides implicit phase completion targets
